content: Just as the language education definition of reflexion frames it as a core learning skill that students actively practice to turn implicit experience into explicit, reusable knowledge, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to adopt the structured scaffolding approach used to teach reflexion in high school writing workshops, rather than relying solely on ad-hoc computational perspective taking. This structured scaffolding can guide LLMs to progressively refine their outputs the same way teachers guide students to revise writing through iterative, guided self-reflection on tone, logic, and audience alignment.
